results.title: Tinker — Wikipedia
results.judge_reason: Source-domain reference about tinkering; describes the dam mechanically but no LLM application, so functionally unrelated to the dam-vanishing patching claim.

results.title: Tinker's Damn phrase
results.judge_reason: Etymology/phrase source-domain only; no LLM editing content, not a functional match.

results.title: A Tinker's Dam — Shelby County Today
results.judge_reason: Source-domain article only; not LLM-related, so cannot be a functional prior art hit.

results.title: SEAL: Self-Adapting LLMs (2025/6)
results.judge_reason: SEAL produces persistent SFT weight-only updates explicitly without retaining auxiliary adaptation modules — this matches the dam-vanishing invariant of the candidate (auxiliary removed, weights changed in place).

results.title: Patching LLM like Software (2025/11)
results.judge_reason: Uses a learnable prefix that persists at inference — opposite of dam-vanishing; the candidate itself flags this paper as a non-match; keyword overlap exists but functional core differs.

results.title: Forward Replay LLM Parameter Editing (2026/5)
results.judge_reason: Concerns specificity/preservation under edits, but does not implement an auxiliary infrastructure that vanishes post-edit; insufficient overlap.

results.title: Massive Editing via Dynamic Weight Generation (2025/12)
results.judge_reason: Dynamic weight generation produces edits but typically retains hypernetwork-style infrastructure; differs from the auxiliary-classifier-then-prune invariant.

results.title: Selective Contextual Reasoning vs Model Editing (2025/3)
results.judge_reason: Proposes contextual reasoning as an alternative to weight editing — opposite direction from dam-vanishing weight patching; not a match.
